latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is the plan for Sign in with valid credentials.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: On the Rivvy Portal UI Login screen (/login), users enter email, password, and optional Remember me. Rivvy Portal Services (Portal API) authenticates against a local password store. If the account is ACTIVE and credentials are valid, the user is signed in. Post-auth redirect behavior: if login was triggered by a protected deep link, return to the originally requested URL; otherwise route by role—global Admin to /admin; Client or Producer to /dashboard. When Remember me is checked, the session is sliding and persists across browser restarts for up to 180 days since last activity (and is invalidated upon explicit sign-out). Without Remember me, the session uses a 30-minute idle-based timeout.

in: Login form in Rivvy Portal UI (/login) with email, password, and Remember me
Local password authentication via Rivvy Portal Services (Portal API)
Role-based routing: global Admin -> /admin; Client or Producer -> /dashboard
Deep-link return: redirect to originally requested protected URL after successful sign-in
Session behavior: Remember me = sliding 180-day persistence across restarts; No Remember me = 30-minute idle-based timeout
Secure session cookie issuance and server-side session store
out: User registration, password reset, and account recovery
Account lockout, rate limiting, and CAPTCHA
Admin or dashboard feature content beyond routing
Sign-out UI/API (session model will support revocation but delivery is separate)

assumptions: Global Admin takes precedence over any other roles for routing.
Client and Producer roles both route to /dashboard.
Non-remember session timeout is 30 minutes of inactivity (sliding).
Remember me is sliding up to 180 days since last activity and is invalidated on explicit sign-out across devices.
If no valid deep link is present (missing, invalid, or external), fall back to role-based landing.
Local password store and hashing (e.g., bcrypt) are already in place for UserAccount.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an ACTIVE account with valid email and password, when I submit the form on /login, then I am signed in and routed to /admin if I have the global Admin role, otherwise to /dashboard if I am a Client or Producer.

Given I checked Remember me, when I sign in successfully, then my session persists across browser restarts and remains valid with activity for up to 180 days since my last activity, and otherwise expires.
Given I did not check Remember me, when I sign in successfully, then my session expires after 30 minutes of inactivity.
Given I attempted to access a protected URL and was redirected to /login, when I sign in successfully, then I am redirected to the originally requested URL.
Given my account is not ACTIVE, when I submit valid-looking credentials, then I am not signed in and receive an appropriate error.
Given my credentials are invalid, when I submit the form, then I am not signed in and receive a generic invalid credentials error.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Sign in with valid credentials

increments.title: Implement email/password sign-in with role-based/deep-link routing and remember-me sessions
increments.intent:
Architecture anchors:
- UI Screen: Login (/login) — ui_screens::ui-mmkz7g4w-3dfdc under Rivvy Portal UI (services::svc-mlxwlakm-ukz3q)
- Backend: Rivvy Portal Services (services::svc-mlxwld2d-zzncf) exposing Portal API (interfaces::ifc-mm2fbrdg-s9zlg)
- Data: user_account (pde-mmq60ts2-m459u), role (pde-mmq60ts2-cgbgt), org_role_assignment (pde-mmq60ts2-twp3t), organization_member (pde-mmq60ts2-zx72q)

Backend (Portal API) — Authentication endpoint and session management:
1) Add POST /auth/login
- Request: { email: string, password: string, rememberMe: boolean, returnTo?: string }
- Response (200): { success: true, redirectTo: string }
- Response (400/401): { success: false, error: 'INVALID_CREDENTIALS' | 'ACCOUNT_INACTIVE' }
- Behavior: normalize email (trim, lowercase) and validate; lookup UserAccount by email; require status === 'ACTIVE' else ACCOUNT_INACTIVE; verify password using local hash (e.g., bcrypt) else INVALID_CREDENTIALS; compute redirectTo: prefer sanitized internal returnTo (must start with '/') else role-based (/admin if global Admin else /dashboard); create server-side session with sliding expiration (sessionId, userId, rememberMe, createdAt, lastActivityAt, idleTimeoutMs = rememberMe ? 180 days : 30 minutes, userAgent, ip, revoked=false); set HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax cookie (name=rp_sess). Cookie attributes: rememberMe=true => Max-Age=180 days; rememberMe=false => session cookie (no Max-Age). Update user_account.last_login_at = now().

2) Add middleware/utilities
- authSessionMiddleware: reads rp_sess cookie, loads session, enforces idle timeout (now - lastActivityAt <= idleTimeoutMs), refreshes lastActivityAt on use (sliding). On expired or revoked, clear cookie and return 401 (for API) or redirect to /login with returnTo. Optionally rotate sessionId periodically.
- roleResolver: helper isGlobalAdmin(userId) using global role assignment; if true => Admin, else treat as Client/Producer.

3) Add GET /auth/me to hydrate client
- Returns { userId, email, roles: string[], isAdmin: boolean } for a valid session (uses middleware).

4) Security considerations
- Keep INVALID_CREDENTIALS generic to avoid enumeration; ACCOUNT_INACTIVE allowed per AC.
- CSRF: SameSite=Lax on session cookie; if framework CSRF is enabled, include token on POST /auth/login.
- Validate/sanitize returnTo to allow only internal relative paths.
- Log auth.login.success/failure with minimal PII (email hash), IP, and userAgent.

5) Configuration
- ENV: SESSION_COOKIE_NAME=rp_sess, SESSION_IDLE_MINUTES=30, REMEMBER_IDLE_DAYS=180, COOKIE_SECURE=true (non-local), COOKIE_DOMAIN, SESSION_STORE=redis|db, SESSION_ROTATE_INTERVAL_MIN=60.

Frontend (Rivvy Portal UI) — Login screen and navigation:
1) Update Login screen (ui-mmkz7g4w-3dfdc)
- Form fields: email (type=email), password, Remember me (checkbox), submit.
- Preserve returnTo from /login?returnTo=... and include in POST /auth/login.
- On success: window.location.assign(redirectTo).
- On error: show inline message ('Invalid email or password' or 'Your account is not active').
- Accessibility: labeled fields, aria-live for errors, disable submit while pending, Enter key support.

2) Route guard for protected pages
- If not authenticated (401 from /auth/me or middleware), redirect to /login?returnTo=encodeURIComponent(currentPathAndQuery).
- If an authenticated user hits /login, redirect them to role-based landing or sanitized returnTo if present.

Routing logic
- Without returnTo: Admin -> /admin; else -> /dashboard. With returnTo: prefer sanitized returnTo.

Observability
- Emit logs/metrics: auth.login.success, auth.login.failure, auth.session.created, auth.session.expired with tags { rememberMe, isAdmin }.

Testing
- Backend unit: invalid creds, inactive user, success; session creation and cookie attributes for rememberMe true/false; sliding lastActivityAt refresh; 30-min vs 180-day enforcement; returnTo sanitization; Admin precedence.
- Integration/E2E: Admin routes to /admin; non-admin to /dashboard; deep-link roundtrip; remember-me survives restart; non-remember session cookie clears on close and idle expiry enforced; errors displayed for inactive/invalid.

Deployment and rollback
- No schema changes required (assumes password hashes exist). Ensure session store configured (Redis/DB). Feature-flag route guard and /auth/login; disable flag to rollback.

messages.content: Hi—ready to help with the “Sign in with valid credentials” story. I have the product backlog and architecture context available. Are there any specific architecture entities or diagrams you’d like to highlight as most relevant for this work item?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my current understanding; I have 6 questions to confirm details before we lock scope.

Answers to your questions:

Q: Where are credentials validated—local password store in Rivvy Portal Services or an external IdP (e.g., Auth0/Cognito)? If external, do we use a hosted login or resource-owner password flow?
A: local password store

Q: How exactly is 'Admin' determined—global role, per-organization role, or separate flag? If multiple roles exist, what is the exact precedence and does default_org_id affect routing?
A: 'Admin' is a global role

Q: Is the definition of 'active account' solely UserAccount.status = 'ACTIVE', or must email verification or organization membership also be present?
A: just 'ACTIVE'

Q: Is the 180-day Remember me an absolute expiry or sliding with activity? Should sign-out immediately invalidate any persistent tokens across devices?
A: sliding. Yes

Q: What is the specific non-remember session timeout duration, and is it absolute or idle-based?
A: you decide

Q: After successful sign-in from a protected deep link, should we return users to the originally requested URL instead of the role-based landing route?
A: originally requested URL
Thanks for the clarifications—updated scope and criteria below. I’m ready to proceed to spec.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software architect to review this.
